goal.ko: 접속사(그래서/그런데/그리고)를 사용해서 경험을 이야기로 연결할 수 있어요!
goal.en: I can connect a story using conjunctions so, but, and!

sections.title: 그래서 / 그런데 / 그리고
sections.subtitle: Conjunctions — story connectors!
sections.description: These three words connect sentences into a story. They always go at the START of the second sentence!

rule_boxes.label: ➡️ 그래서 = so / therefore
examples: 무릎이 아팠어요. 그래서 무릎을 봤어요.
= My knee hurt. So I looked at it.
배가 고팠어요. 그래서 밥을 먹었어요. = I was hungry, so I ate.

rule_boxes.label: ↔️ 그런데 = but / however / by the way
examples: 스쿠터가 기울어졌어요. 그런데 괜찮았어요.
= The scooter tilted. But I was okay.
다음 날은 무릎에 피딱지가 보였어요. (그런데 = surprise!)

rule_boxes.label: ➕ 그리고 = and / and then
examples: 피딱지가 보였어요. 그리고 진물도 흘렀어요.
= There was a scab. And discharge was oozing too.
공부했어요. 그리고 잠을 잤어요. = I studied. And then I slept.

rule_boxes.label: ⚠️ 그래도 = even so / still
examples: 넘어졌어요. 그래도 스쿠터를 타고 싶어요.
= I fell. Even so, I still want to ride the scooter!
힘들어요. 그래도 할 거예요. = It's hard. Still, I'll do it.

examples.english: My knee hurt. So I looked at it.

examples.korean: 스쿠터가 기울어졌어요. 그런데 다음 날은 더 심해졌어요.
examples.english: The scooter tilted. But the next day it got worse.
A scab formed. And discharge oozed too.

sections.title: -지만
sections.subtitle: but — contrast within one sentence
sections.description: -지만 connects two contrasting ideas IN ONE sentence. Different from 그런데 which starts a new sentence!

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 Verb + 지만 = tries to... but
examples: 뛰어 내리려고 했지만 같이 넘어졌어요.
= I tried to jump off but fell together with it.
공부하지만 재미없어요. = I study but it's not fun.

rule_boxes.label: 🟡 그런데 vs. -지만
examples: 힘들었어요. 그런데 재미있었어요. (2 sentences)
힘들었지만 재미있었어요. (1 sentence)
= Both mean 'It was hard BUT fun.'

examples.english: I tried to jump off but fell with it.
It hurt but I held on.

sections.title: -고 있었어요
sections.subtitle: was doing — past progressive
sections.description: Like -고 있어요 (is doing) but in the PAST. Used for ongoing actions that were happening at a moment in the past.

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 -고 있어요 → -고 있었어요 (past)
examples: 스쿠터를 타고 있어요. = is riding (now)
스쿠터를 타고 있었어요. = was riding (then)
형이 점프를 하고 있었어요. = The older boy was jumping.

examples.english: My sibling and I were riding scooters.

examples.korean: 동네 형이 스쿠터로 점프를 하고 있었어요.
examples.english: The older boy in the neighborhood was jumping with his scooter.

lines.speaker: 리암
lines.en: My sibling and I were riding scooters.

lines.speaker: 리암
lines.text: 동네 형이 스쿠터로 점프를 하고 있었어요.
lines.en: The older boy in the neighborhood was jumping with his scooter.

lines.speaker: 리암
lines.en: I wanted to try copying him too.

lines.speaker: 리암
lines.text: 나는 뛰어 내리려고 했지만 같이 넘어졌어요.
lines.en: I tried to jump off but fell with it.

lines.speaker: 리암
lines.text: 그래서 무릎을 봤어요. 조금 까져 있었어요.
lines.en: So I looked at my knee. It was a bit scraped.

lines.speaker: 리암
lines.en: Even so, I still want to ride the scooter coolly.

fill_items.en: My knee hurt. So I looked at it.
There was a scab. And discharge oozed too.
The scooter tilted. But I tried to jump off.
I fell. Even so, I want to try again!
I tried to jump off but fell.

step1_chunks.english: My sibling and I were riding scooters.

step1_chunks.sentence: 나는 뛰어 내리려고 했지만 같이 넘어졌어요.
step1_chunks.english: I tried to jump off but fell with it.

step1_chunks.sentence: 그래서 무릎을 봤어요. 조금 까져 있었어요.
step1_chunks.english: So I looked at my knee. It was a bit scraped.

step2_words.english: My sibling and I were riding scooters.

step2_words.sentence: 그런데 다음 날은 무릎에 피딱지가 보였어요.
step2_words.english: But the next day a scab appeared on my knee.
Even so, I still want to ride the scooter coolly.

write_items.question: 🛴 넘어졌어요. ___ 무릎이 아팠어요. (so/therefore)
write_items.question_en: I fell. ___ my knee hurt. (use: 그래서)
write_items.answer_en: I fell. So my knee hurt.

write_items.question: 🎯 한 가지 경험을 그래서/그런데/그리고로 연결해서 써봐요.
write_items.question_en: Write about one experience using 그래서, 그런데, or 그리고.
write_items.answer: 배가 고팠어요. 그래서 밥을 먹었어요. 그런데 아직도 배가 고팠어요!
write_items.answer_en: I was hungry. So I ate. But I was still hungry!

lines.en: I fell off my scooter. So my knee got scraped.
Yes, but I am okay now!

lines.kr: 친구랑 놀았어요. 그리고 같이 밥도 먹었어요.
lines.en: I played with a friend. And we ate together too.
Yes! But I got home late.

self_check.en: I can use '그래서' to connect cause and result.
I can use '그런데' for contrast or a surprise turn.
I can use '그리고' to connect events in order.

self_check.ko: '-지만'으로 한 문장 안에서 대조를 표현할 수 있어요.
self_check.en: I can use '-지만' for contrast within one sentence.

self_check.ko: '-고 있었어요'로 과거에 하고 있던 일을 말할 수 있어요.
self_check.en: I can say what was happening in the past with '-고 있었어요'.
